speaker: doctor
text: Good morning. I see your body temperature is quite high, 39.4 degrees Celsius. Are you experiencing any fever symptoms?

speaker: doctor
text: Also, your blood glucose level came back elevated at 13.4 mmol/L, which we should definitely discuss.

speaker: patient
text: Yes, I've been feeling very warm and sweaty since last night.

speaker: doctor
text: I understand. Let's check your blood pressure as well.

speaker: doctor
text: Your systolic blood pressure is 139 mmHg, which is slightly elevated but not critical at this moment.

speaker: patient
text: I've also been feeling nauseous on and off today.

speaker: doctor
text: Given your elevated blood sugar and symptoms, I am diagnosing you with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.

speaker: patient
text: I see. What should I do about the nausea and the high sugar levels?

speaker: doctor
text: I am going to start you on Lisinopril 20mg once daily to help control your blood pressure and protect your kidneys.

speaker: patient
text: Is Lisinopril going to help with my blood sugar too?

speaker: doctor
text: Lisinopril mainly manages blood pressure, but controlling it can indirectly benefit your diabetes management.

speaker: doctor
text: By the way, I rechecked your blood glucose and it is now 10.8 mmol/L, which is better but still above target.

speaker: patient
text: Actually, this morning my blood sugar was even higher than that earlier reading you mentioned.

speaker: doctor
text: That variability can happen, but we'll work on stabilizing it with medications and lifestyle changes.

speaker: doctor
text: Given your blood pressure readings, I am going to reduce your Lisinopril dose to 10mg once daily to avoid low blood pressure.

speaker: doctor
text: Your systolic blood pressure has risen to 152 mmHg, which is concerning and needs close monitoring.

speaker: patient
text: I'm still running a fever of 39.4 degrees Celsius, just like when I first came in.

speaker: doctor
text: Actually, your temperature has increased to 40.0 degrees Celsius now. We need to address this urgently.

speaker: patient
text: What should I do about this high fever? Should I take anything for it?

speaker: doctor
text: I recommend taking antipyretics like acetaminophen and increasing your fluid intake. If the fever persists, we may need further investigations.
